title: T-026 — Partial-move split cascade (WH §8.2)
prompt:
# T-026 — Partial-move split cascade (WH §8.2)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §8.2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Implement the §8.2 cascade: when `move_qty < LP.qty`, the service splits source into child (move_qty, moved to dest) + parent (remainder, stays at source). New LP is the tracked entity in stock_moves; parent retains stable identity.

## Implementation contract
1. Integration in `apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/stock-move.ts` (or sibling `stock-move-cascade.ts`): when `move_qty < lp.quantity`, internally invoke `splitLicensePlate({outputs: [{qty: move_qty, destination_location_id: to}, {qty: remainder, destination_location_id: from}]})` then return the moved child LP id.
2. Single stock_moves row references the new child LP (per §8.2 rationale — fresh history).
3. lp_genealogy captures split + implicit move relationship.

## Files
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/stock-move-split-cascade.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/stock-move-split-cascade.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given LP of 100 BOX, when 40 moved to LocB, then a child LP of 40 BOX exists at LocB, parent LP of 60 BOX remains at original location, stock_moves row references child LP, and 2 genealogy rows exist.
2. Given `move_qty == lp.quantity`, when moved, then NO split occurs and the LP itself moves (no cascade).
3. Given move_qty > lp.quantity, when called, then service rejects with V-WH-MOV-002.

## Test strategy
- RED: `apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/stock-move-cascade.test.ts` asserting split-on-partial, no-split-on-full, qty-exceeds rejection.
- Run `pnpm --filter web test stock-move-cascade`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not split on full-qty move.
- Do not write stock_moves referencing the parent LP for the moved qty.
- Do not skip genealogy.

## Dependencies
- T-017
- T-025

PO/TO/WO read models, RM root reservations, WO release/cancel hooks
